Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Approving Receiver'S Final Account And Request; Final Account
Off Calendar Aug-16-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael A. Isaacs, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge, Tel. 415.356.4600, for receiver and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RECEIVER DAVID A. BRADLOW's Motion For Order Approving Receiver's Final Account And Request for: (1) Compensation & Expense Reimbursement, (2) Exoneration of Bond, and (3) Discharge of Receiver is Granted. A statement of non-opposition was filed by Plaintiff on August 7, 2012. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Confirm Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jun-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL A. ISAACS OF MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP, TEL. NO. 356-4600 APPEARING FOR THE RECEIVER;
ATTY. RONALD D. SCHIVO OF UHL, TEL. NO. 925-1488 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DAVID BRADLOW'S Motion For Order To Confirm Sale Of Real Property IS Granted. The facts and circumstances of this case support judicial confirmation of the sale of the property. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

J. Brian McCauley, Esq., Law Office, 415-974-1515, for moving party

WAYNE YEE, DEFENDANT APPEARING WITHOUT HIS ATTORNEY.

No opposition filed. MR. YEE WAS REQUIRED TO GIVE HIS E-MAIL ADDRESS TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SINCE HE DOES NOT HAVE ANY ADDRESS ON FILE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Ntc Of Mtn For Order Vacating Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WAYNE LEE Motion for Order Vacating Default Judgment. Appearances: Jill L. Cannon, Esq. Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3820, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; J. Brian McCauley, Attorney at Law, (415) 974-1515, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Usual two year requirement has been met, Court looked at all documents and finds service valid. Also, the Court sustains objec. to Defendant's Exh. 9; and Court takes Jud. Ntc. of the 2000 Census. (Counsel for CCSF to prepare Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc Of Mtn For Order Vacating Default Judgment

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2005 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Dec-02-2004 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerks: S. Ravinantapricha/ R. Edwards, CSR: Tony Vaughn.
Niall Vignoles, Esq. for Plaintiff: Gary Ho for CCSF. present, sworn and examined.

After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant: Wayne S. Yee. Monetary Judgment for attorney fees, costs, and civil penalties; and an Injunction are signed in open court. Terms of the judgment is outlined in the judgment.
